title: 'jjf push' from a fresh clone exits 1, forever (meta/format-version non-fast-forward)
body:
# Symptom

Every `jjf push origin` from a fresh clone exits 1 — and never
self-heals. The actual issue/memory data pushes successfully,
but the push refspec also tries to push the local
`refs/jjf/meta/format-version`, which is non-fast-forward
against the remote's.

# Repro

```
$ jjf push origin
jjf: push to origin rejected: ...
 ! [rejected]  refs/jjf/meta/format-version
   -> refs/jjf/meta/format-version (non-fast-forward)
$ echo $?
1
# Repeat after `jjf pull origin` — still exit 1.
# Repeat after any local change — still exit 1, forever.
```

# Root cause

The push refspec is `refs/jjf/*:refs/jjf/*`. The local
`format-version` commit (written by `jjf init`) diverges from
the remote's; non-fast-forward rejection sticks the exit code
at 1 even when all data successfully pushed.

# Fix candidate

Exclude `meta/*` from the push refspec (same root cause as
F-002 — same fix probably resolves both). Or stop tracking
`format-version` as a writable ref altogether (see F-010).

# Cross-links

Same cluster: F-001 / F-002 / F-010.

# Confidence

Reproducible 100% on every fresh clone, on every push.
